policy_refs: [BNK-CC-RULE-001, BNK-CC-RULE-002, BNK-CC-RULE-003, BNK-CC-RULE-004, BNK-CC-RULE-005, BNK-CC-RULE-006, BNK-SEC-RULE-001, BNK-SEC-RULE-002, BNK-REG-RULE-001, BNK-REG-RULE-002, BNK-CC-RULE-020, BNK-CC-RULE-021, BNK-CC-RULE-022, BNK-CC-RULE-023]
expected_outcome: Customer is the control person on a commercial real estate LLC and wants to add her brother as an authorized signer (not a beneficial owner — he won't own equity). Andre verifies her as the control person, explains what the change requires (in-person ID verification for the new signer at a branch since he is not present on this call), opens an Authorized Signer Change ticket, schedules the brother's branch visit. PASS — clean SOP. No CIP/Beneficial Ownership trigger (no ownership change), but the new signer must still complete CIP at the branch.

coverage.element: Third-party (new signer) NOT made changes for over the phone
CIP for new signer scheduled (branch in-person)
Ownership-change distinction explained (NOT a Beneficial Owner)

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling Meridian Trust Bank. My name is Andre, how may I help you today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Please note this call is recorded for quality assurance and regulatory compliance purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Andre, this is Helena Voss with Voss Commercial Holdings LLC. I'd like to add my brother as an authorized signer on our business account.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hello, Ms. Voss. Happy to help walk through that with you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you are calling regarding adding an authorized signer to your business account, and I can certainly help. Because this is a business-account change, I'll need to verify you as the control person on file first.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Of course.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Could you give me the business account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Eight-eight-two-one, four-four-zero-seven, six-six.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the full legal business name as registered with the state?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Voss Commercial Holdings LLC, organized in Illinois.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your full legal name as it appears on the formation documents?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Helena Marie Voss.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Date of birth?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: October eleventh, nineteen-eighty-seven.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: All matches. I have you verified as the control person on the account, and our records show you and your mother Diane Voss as the two beneficial owners, fifty-fifty. Is that still accurate?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. No change in ownership.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. Tell me about the change you want to make — adding your brother. Just to confirm a couple of things up front: he would be an authorized signer, meaning he can sign for the LLC and operate the account, but he is not becoming an owner — correct?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Correct. He's not buying in. He just helps us with day-to-day operations and we want him to be able to deposit rent checks and pay vendors.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Understood, and that's a very common arrangement. Because he's not becoming an owner, this is just an authorized signer change — not a beneficial ownership update — which keeps the process a bit lighter on the regulatory side.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What does the process look like?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Here's the part I have to be transparent about. Even though we're not changing ownership, your brother does still need to be screened and ID-verified by us as the new signer — that's our Customer Identification Program requirement. And because he isn't on the call right now and isn't currently listed as an authorized party, I can't make changes for him over the phone today. He'll need to come into a branch in person with a government-issued photo ID.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That makes sense. I figured.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And one brief standard statement — your information is used solely to provide and service your accounts, in accordance with our Privacy Notice and applicable law.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Understood.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So here's what I propose. I'll start the Authorized Signer Change ticket on this call. I'll capture your authorization as the control person — that part I can do over the phone since you are verified — and the ticket will sit open pending your brother's in-branch ID verification.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: What's your brother's full legal name?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Martin Edward Voss.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. When he visits the branch, he'll need to bring a current driver's license or passport — current government-issued photo ID. They'll capture his date of birth, address, SSN, and run our CIP screen including the OFAC check. Once that clears — which is typically same-day — the signer change is activated and he can transact on the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Does he need to bring anything specific from the company side, or is the photo ID enough?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Photo ID and his Social Security card if he has it handy. The system will already see that he's the authorized-signer add tied to your LLC, so the branch banker can pull up the existing ticket. He doesn't need to bring anything from the company.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: One more clarification on the ownership distinction — because Martin is not becoming a beneficial owner, the company's ownership filings with us don't change. You and your mother remain the two fifty-percent beneficial owners. If at any point he were to acquire even a small ownership interest — five percent, ten percent, anything — we'd need to refile beneficial ownership under the Corporate Transparency Act. For now, no refile needed.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: He won't be acquiring ownership. That's been the discussion. He gets a salary, not equity.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Then we're clean. Authorized signer only.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Opening the Authorized Signer Change ticket now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Ticket is open and references the addition of Martin Edward Voss as an authorized signer, with your authorization on file.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wonderful.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Where's the most convenient branch for Martin?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: He's near the Skokie branch. He's free Thursday morning.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Scheduling a Thursday morning appointment at the Skokie branch — they'll see he has an open Authorized Signer Change ticket when he checks in. Confirmation will email to the address on file for the company, and Martin should receive an appointment-confirmation email at the email you provide for him in a moment.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Martin's email is m-voss at voss-commercial dot com.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Captured. Confirmation will go out immediately.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: you called to add your brother Martin Voss as an authorized signer on the Voss Commercial Holdings business account. I opened the Authorized Signer Change ticket with your authorization as control person, scheduled a Thursday morning branch appointment at the Skokie location for Martin to complete CIP and ID verification in person. Once that clears — usually same-day — Martin will be active on the account. Ownership remains unchanged. Does that all sound right, Ms. Voss?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that's exactly what I was hoping to hear. Thank you, Andre.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My pleasure. Your case reference number is M-T-B dash two-eight-four-four-zero-zero-three. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — appreciate the feedback if you have a moment. Thanks for banking with Meridian.
